conjectures.label: upper-rate
conjectures.current_result: The full-class no-log upper-rate claim remains refuted. A primitive known-logging finite-cell upper theorem is proved: sup_P E R <= C n^{-r_*(alpha,gamma)} (log n)^{(1+alpha)/(2+alpha)} on kappa-uniform primitive finite-cell feasible collections. The upper collection excludes one-sided overlap decay; gamma enters only through the deterministic clipping sequence q_n^*.
conjectures.approach: Achievability is scoped to a primitive regime where nuisance bounds are derived: logging propensity is known exactly on a known finite partition, outcome regressions are cell-constant, foldwise truncated empirical cell means are used, and a rare-arm cell-count envelope Lambda_n(P) implies local clipped-layer outcome L1 accuracy. Known logging removes the q^{-2} estimated-clipping variance term, leaving the ordinary q^{-1} finite-VC fixed point.
conjectures.key_gap: No upper theorem is proved over the larger weak-overlap class with unknown nuisance functions. The exact unknown-logging data-only feasible minimax rate under ordinary L2 propensity control remains open.

conjectures.label: lower-calibration
conjectures.current_result: The two-point lower bound at exponent r_*(alpha,gamma) is proved on the full weak-overlap class using only the endpoint witness beta=beta_{alpha,gamma}. The witness places the sign-varying outcome regression on the weak arm, so chi-square information is h^{2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}}.
conjectures.approach: The Le Cam construction uses active mass h^alpha, weak-arm probability h^{beta_{alpha,gamma}}, contrast h, and n h^{2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}}=1. The endpoint primitive-cube assumption now states exactly the witness family consumed by the lower theorem. The known-logging finite-cell witness also satisfies the primitive finite-cell nuisance structure because the active weak-arm count is h^{-2} and the cell-count envelope is bounded by h^{1+alpha} L^{(1+alpha)/(2+alpha)}.

assumptions_live.name: ass:nuisance
assumptions_live.note: Primitive known-logging finite-cell structure: known partition, known logging propensity, cell-constant outcome regressions, empirical cell-mean learners, and an explicit rare-arm cell-count envelope Lambda_n(P). It does not include overlap decay.

assumptions_live.name: def:uniform-feasible-collection
assumptions_live.note: Denotes kappa-uniform primitive finite-cell feasible collections satisfying causal sampling, margin, policy class, nuisance structure, and common fold constants. It explicitly excludes ass:overlap-decay from the upper-achievability definition.

assumptions_live.name: lem:primitive-nuisance-derived
assumptions_live.note: Derives foldwise good events and local clipped-layer outcome L1 accuracy from bounded-outcome concentration and the primitive cell-count envelope.

assumptions_live.name: lem:drift-derived
assumptions_live.note: The clipped-score drift is derived from known logging and the local cell-mean outcome accuracy. No product nuisance or target-rate L2 propensity assumption is used.

assumptions_live.name: lem:feasible-vc-offset
assumptions_live.note: With known logging, the conditional second moment on a disagreement set is q^{-1} P_X(D); the q^{-2} integral estimated-clipping term is absent. The proof consumes margin and finite VC conditions, not overlap decay.

assumptions_live.name: ass:overlap-decay
assumptions_live.note: Defines the weak-overlap law class and calibrates the lower witness. It is not part of the primitive finite-cell upper theorem.

assumptions_live.name: ass:calibrated-overlap-richness
assumptions_live.note: Retained as the condition under which the delta_{alpha,gamma}-calibrated motivating witness is overlap-admissible. The main lower theorem uses beta_{alpha,gamma}.

assumptions_live.name: ass:primitive-cube
assumptions_live.note: Now states only the endpoint beta=beta_{alpha,gamma} witness actually consumed by thm:lower-full-class. The unused requirement of witnesses for every 0 <= beta <= beta_{alpha,gamma} has been removed.

assumptions_live.name: ass:learnable-cube
assumptions_live.note: Known-logging finite-cell endpoint witnesses are shown to satisfy the primitive finite-cell nuisance structure.

assumptions_live.name: cor:feasible-bracket model-class clause
assumptions_live.note: The corollary no longer requires Q subseteq P_{alpha,gamma}. The upper bound uses primitive finite-cell feasibility; the lower bound uses explicit containment of the two witness laws in Q. Subclassing Q inside P_{alpha,gamma} is only interpretive.

assumptions_live.name: finite-VC logarithmic multiplier
assumptions_live.note: The ordinary finite-VC entropy factor remains in the known-logging upper theorem. No logarithmic lower bound is claimed from finite VC dimension alone.

tried_and_failed.what: Pooled cross-fit decomposition using a single training sigma-field.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: No single global conditioning event makes all pooled validation observations independent of all fitted nuisance functions; each fold must be conditioned on its own training sigma-field.

tried_and_failed.what: Keeping the feasible clipped-score theorem by assuming target-order feasible nuisance offsets.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: Those are estimator-determined offset bounds, not primitive law-side or learner-side assumptions.

tried_and_failed.what: Concluding a uniform full-class upper bound from margin, overlap decay, finite VC complexity, and bounded outcomes alone.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The full weak-overlap law class imposes no smoothness, sparsity, finite-dimensional structure, known-logging mechanism, or finite-cell cell-count envelope guaranteeing the needed nuisance rates.

tried_and_failed.what: Claiming the finite-VC logarithmic multiplier is unavoidable under finite VC dimension alone.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: Finite VC dimension is only an upper-complexity condition and includes finite or two-policy classes where a logarithmic entropy lower bound is false.

tried_and_failed.what: Claiming the old beta <= 2 matched theorem under only L2 propensity control.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The estimated-score second moment has an extra q^{-2} int_D |ehat-e_P| term. Cauchy-Schwarz gives a stochastic radius that dominates the claimed fixed point when beta_{alpha,gamma} > 1 + alpha/2, and adds a larger logarithmic power at equality.

tried_and_failed.what: Using an active-block lower witness without specifying that the perturbed arm is weak.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: If the sign-varying outcome regression is placed on the strong arm, the chi-square information is h^{2+alpha} rather than h^{2+alpha+beta}, so the Le Cam calibration fails.

tried_and_failed.what: Using empirical off-block propensity estimates in the learnable lower witnesses for all beta.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The off-block propensity L2 error creates a weighted product h^{2+alpha-beta/2}, too large when beta > 2. The compatible witness proof keeps known logging on the finite witness partition.

tried_and_failed.what: Closing the estimated-clipping variance term by assuming ||ehat-e_P||_{2,P_X} <= C rho_n with rho_n equal to the target feasible rate.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: This is not a primitive achievable condition. In some regimes it would require faster-than-root-n uniform L2 propensity accuracy without any smoothness, sparsity, finite-dimensional design, or known-logging condition.

tried_and_failed.what: Stating the known-logging finite-cell upper theorem through a feasible-collection definition that also required overlap decay.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The reproduced upper proof never consumes the overlap-tail inequality; carrying ass:overlap-decay in the upper definition was a redundant law-side restriction.

tried_and_failed.what: Stating thm:lower-full-class under a primitive-cube assumption requiring witnesses for every beta in [0,beta_{alpha,gamma}].
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: The lower proof consumes only the endpoint witness beta=beta_{alpha,gamma}: m_h asymp h^alpha, q_h=h^{beta_{alpha,gamma}}, chi^2=O(h^{2+alpha+beta_{alpha,gamma}}), and regret h^{1+alpha}.

tried_and_failed.what: Stating cor:feasible-bracket with Q subseteq P_{alpha,gamma} as a hypothesis.
tried_and_failed.why_it_failed: Neither reproduced inequality consumes Q subseteq P_{alpha,gamma}: the upper bound uses primitive finite-cell feasibility, and the lower bound uses explicit containment of P_{n,+},P_{n,-} in Q.

open_subgoals.what_is_needed: Either a primitive clipping-stability condition or a sharper estimator/proof controlling the q^{-2} estimated-clipping variance term without assuming target-rate L2 propensity accuracy.

open_subgoals.goal: Upper theorem over the larger weak-overlap class
open_subgoals.what_is_needed: Primitive law-side smoothness, design, sparsity, finite-dimensional, known-logging, or other learner assumptions that imply the required clipped-layer nuisance control uniformly for every law in the intended class.
A primitive local-complexity condition stronger than finite VC entropy, such as finite-class Massart structure or a no-log local entropy integral.

open_subgoals.goal: Matching logarithmic lower bound for rich finite-VC policy classes
open_subgoals.what_is_needed: A primitive many-location richness condition compatible with the margin and overlap envelopes, plus a Fano or Assouad construction that yields the relevant logarithmic exponent.

Concrete smoothness, sparsity, finite-dimensional, or design assumptions plus specified nuisance learners that imply local weak-arm outcome accuracy and propensity clipping stability from primitive quantities.

round_summary: The lower richness assumption was reduced to the endpoint witness actually consumed by the Le Cam proof, and all witness notation was coordinated around q(h) and e_h. The feasible-bracket corollary no longer assumes Q subseteq P_{alpha,gamma}; its lower half depends only on explicit containment of the two witness laws. The upper theorem, lower theorem, and polynomial feasible bracket are preserved; unknown-logging full-class achievability and exact logarithmic matching remain open.
